growth_records.medium: modified Allen mineral base medium + 0.2% w/v tryptone
growth_records.cite: Andersson et al. Global analysis of mRNA stability in the archaeon Sulfolobus. Genome Biology (2006). DOI:10.1186/gb-2006-7-10-r99. [PMC1794556]
growth_records.snippet: S. solfataricus DSM1617 and S. acidocaldarius DSM639 ... cultures were grown at 79°C in modified Allen mineral base medium containing 0.2% w/v tryptone... Growth was monitored by optical density (OD) measurements at 600
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (6 h) reported directly in Materials and Methods (exponential-phase batch culture, OD600 monitoring). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/td = ln(2)/6 = 0.1155 h^-1. Oxygen status inferred as aerobic (standard for Sulfolobus grown in shaken Erlenmeyer flasks); pH not stated in text.

growth_records.cite: Barillà et al. Driving Apart and Segregating Genomes in Archaea. Trends in Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1016/j.tim.2016.07.001. [PMC5120986]
growth_records.snippet: members of the thermophilic genus Sulfolobus, such as Sulfolobus solfataricus (doubling time ∼425 minutes) and Sulfolobus acidocaldarius (doubling time ∼213 minutes)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~425 min = 7.083 h) reported in this review (citing prior cell-cycle studies by the Bernander group). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/7.083 = 0.0979 /h. This is a review article; no specific medium, temperature or pH for the growth experiment underlying this number is stated in the text, so those fields are left null rather than assumed from general knowledge of Sulfolobus culturing.
